items.title: Bridget Archer standing in for Rockliff is a soft-launched succession plan

children.content: Make no mistake, news that maverick Liberal Bridget Archer will be acting Tasmanian premier for a week is a significant step towards her eventually taking over from Jeremy Rockliff — a move sure to enrage those who believe they have a more rightful claim.

gallery.altText: Bridget Archer and Jeremy Rockliff sit next to each other on a park bench.

thumbnail.altText: Bridget Archer and Jeremy Rockliff sit next to each other on a park bench.

items.title: Online bookmaker Betr shifts operations from NT to Tasmania

children.content: With sports bookmaker Betr granted a licence to operate in Tasmania, independent MP Andrew Wilkie slams the government for "rolling out the red carpet" despite the state admitting its regulations are not up to scratch.

image.altText: Gambling app on mobile phone screen.

items.title: Man behind a bold CBD plan may have been running insolvent companies

children.content: The Tasmanian government and a local council welcomed a bold plan to transform Launceston’s CBD from a man who may have been running insolvent companies at the time, an investigation shows.

image.altText: A man in a jacket and white shirt.

items.title: Bars getting 'squeezed' as costs go up and Hobartians can't pay, tourism body says

children.content: There are questions around the future of Hobart's nightlife after the abrupt closure of some of the capital city's most-loved institutions due to challenging trading conditions.

gallery.altText: Two men carry metal chairs through the front door of a pub, with the sign 'Jack Greene' above the door.
A female with a blonde pixie haircut in a black tee and pants stands outside the Observatory Bar, hands in her pockets.
A nigh club filled with people dancing under the purple and green lights.

thumbnail.altText: Two men carry metal chairs through the front door of a pub, with the sign 'Jack Greene' above the door.
A female with a blonde pixie haircut in a black tee and pants stands outside the Observatory Bar, hands in her pockets.
A nigh club filled with people dancing under the purple and green lights.

items.title: New Australian air pressure record brings deep freeze to south-east

children.content: Preliminary data shows the highest ever pressure in Australia was recorded in Tasmania on Monday morning.

image.altText: the yarra valley in victoria covered in thick frost

items.title: Neil the seal is 1 tonne right now. What happens when he's 4 tonnes?

children.content: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania, as well as a growing social media audience, but fears for his wellbeing and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance have led to calls to better protect Neil.

image.altText: An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

items.title: Jai Arrow marries partner months after MND diagnosis

children.content: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

gallery.altText: A man and woman kiss at their wedding.
A man holding a baby and looking at a woman in a wedding dress sitting on stairs.
A man and woman in wedding clothing holding a baby and smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman kiss at their wedding.
A man holding a baby and looking at a woman in a wedding dress sitting on stairs.
A man and woman in wedding clothing holding a baby and smiling.

items.title: Richard White resigns as WiseTech chair but will remain with firm

children.content: The billionaire founder of WiseTech Global said he was stepping down as the company's chair after "recent personal media attention" created an "unnecessary distraction" from the business.

image.altText: Close up of Richard White smiling on stage.

items.title: Pam the Bird graffiti vandal detained after bridge stand-off

children.content: A trespasser has been detained by police after spending many hours at the top of a 140- metre-high tower of the Bolte Bridge in Melbourne where he'd spray-painted the surface with the notorious graffiti symbol.

image.altText: A man dressed in a black top and pants walks along the rocks protruding from a river with his hands in the air.

items.title: After months of Trump threats, Cuba says he's committing 'genocide'

children.content: The lives of ordinary Cubans have ground to a halt under an economic and humanitarian crisis that has developed over years and been aggravated by US President Donald Trump's harsh restrictions, experts say.

gallery.altText: A blue tricycle with a trailer driving past colourful buildings and a 'Cuba' sign.

thumbnail.altText: A blue tricycle with a trailer driving past colourful buildings and a 'Cuba' sign.

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra 'overwhelmed by the kindness' after truck crash

children.content: Former Adelaide Crows and Fremantle player Tony Modra says he has left hospital after he was seriously injured in a truck crash last month.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Origin legacies and futures on the line for NSW in decider

children.content: As New South Wales look to rise from the ashes and pull off a Lang Park Origin heist, the future of the team and the legacy of some of its greatest players will be tested in the furnace of a decider.

image.altText: New South Wales huddle up at training ahead of Origin III

items.title: Families told accused Bali bomber Hambali to face 202 life terms

children.content: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

image.altText: A bespectacled middle aged Indonesian man with a grey beard but no moustache wears a black and white top.

items.title: Government commits to fix dam that 'cannot be repaired'

children.content: The Queensland government announces a plan to rebuild the Paradise Dam wall where it is, despite its operator previously saying there were too many issues to fix it.

image.altText: aerial shots of Paradise Dam

items.title: Sick of paying for streaming services? These films and TV series are free

children.content: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

items.title: What you need to know before claiming your phone as a tax deduction

children.content: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

image.altText: Close up view of young women using a smartphone on a couch in living room.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

image.altText: A cow, a pig and a horse are cut into a corn field, seen from above.

items.title: 'Disappointing': Prince Harry and king's awkward stand-off over palace

children.content: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.

gallery.altText: A redheaded man with a beard

thumbnail.altText: A redheaded man with a beard

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: SA premier condemns 'fight club' but teens vow to continue bouts

children.content: South Australia's premier says he is appalled by an amateur children's "fight club", while the state's police minister says people who post videos of the fights online may face legal action on top of the participants in the bouts.

image.altText: Two boys fighting while another two people watch on

items.title: Boys major victims of AI-assisted online sexual abuse, study finds

children.content: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

image.altText: A maths student studies with a calculator on their desk.

items.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

articles.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

image.altText: Two men stand with black t-shirts, one man on the left is playing a VR headset.

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 extra charges after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Ex-champion boxer charged over four kidnappings

children.content: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged 26-year-old Humzah Khalid, previously known by his boxing name The Raging Bull, for his alleged involvement in four kidnappings over 10 months.

image.altText: A man wearing boxing gloves and a black shirt looks serious as he stands in a boxing ring with posters on the walls.

items.title: 'A lot of stigma': The 'taboo' cancer side effects we don't talk about

children.content: Shame and stigma mean gynaecological side effects from cancer treatment — affecting women's sexual, urinary and reproductive health — are under-reported and under-researched.

gallery.altText: A composite image of two women side-by-side.
A woman and a man smile at the camera together. He is kissing the woman's head.
A woman speaks on a podium, she has short curly hair and a serious expression.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of two women side-by-side.
A woman and a man smile at the camera together. He is kissing the woman's head.
A woman speaks on a podium, she has short curly hair and a serious expression.

items.title: Mushroom murder trial judge shares story in podcast

children.content: Retired judge Christopher Beale has reflected on his long legal career, his temporary pivot to becoming a Jesuit priest and the role he played in the Erin Patterson murder trial.

image.altText: A judge wearing traditional court garb speaks into a microphone.

items.title: Democrats' Platner accused of sexual assault, dashing party's US Senate hopes

children.content: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

image.altText: A middle-aged white man with a beard and a sleeve tattoo speaks passionately at a campaign event.

items.title: Melbourne to put on extravaganza rivalling Bollywood to welcome PM Modi

children.content: On a grey July day, a Melbourne community hall is bursting with colour as Australia's Indian community gears up to welcome Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

Smiling women in red blouses, cream sarees, large bindis, hair tied, have hand behind hair as they sing.
A group of women dancers dressed in a form of a saree draw poses, some stand, some sit, all smiling, in a leafy courtyard.
